title: Fix worker startup race in wakeup-path CLI test
description: Address RoboRev finding on tests/cli.rs by replacing the fixed startup sleep in the rapid publish wakeup test with a readiness check before starting the timed sequence.

title: Address roborev findings for example plugin workflow
description: Fix RoboRev findings on the example plugin by sanitizing reflected input, adding direct binary tests for empty and non-empty stdin, and reducing duplicated example workflow steps across docs.

title: Fix worker readiness race in latency measurement script
description: Address RoboRev finding on scripts/measure_latency.py by replacing the fixed startup sleep with a readiness check before measuring request/reply latency.

title: Clarify worker timeout for real Gemini runs
description: Document that plugboard run defaults to a 60-second per-message timeout, that real Gemini runs may take minutes, and improve CLI/help/examples so users set a larger timeout when validating real request/reply workflows.

title: Add request metadata via metadata_json.meta
description: Support repeatable request --meta KEY=VALUE flags by storing them under messages.metadata_json.meta, inject PLUGBOARD_META_* env vars per plugin invocation, and let ollama-plugin honor PLUGBOARD_META_MODEL.

title: Fix Gemini plugin prompt and stdin mapping
description: Update gemini-plugin to preserve the Plugboard stdin contract while invoking Gemini in non-interactive mode with a fixed --prompt instruction, then verify the exact shell invocation against the installed Gemini CLI.
notes:
Implemented the adapter change so Plugboard message bodies remain on stdin while Gemini receives a fixed --prompt instruction and added tests for that contract. Shell validation in this Codex environment remains unresolved: plain 'gemini -p' examples return for the user, but timeout-bounded probes here still hang for the plugin-style JSON and approval-mode invocations.
Scope refined: simplify the Gemini adapter and docs around direct --prompt usage. Plugboard still delivers the message to the plugin on stdin, but the plugin will pass that body to Gemini as --prompt and stop forwarding stdin to the Gemini subprocess.
Simplified the adapter again: it now reads the worker-delivered message body from stdin, passes that body directly as Gemini's --prompt value, and does not forward stdin to the Gemini subprocess. Updated Gemini-specific docs to use the simplest user-facing example, 'gemini --prompt ...'. Cargo tests pass, but timeout-bounded direct Gemini probes from this Codex shell still do not reproduce the user's successful interactive runs.
User confirmed that direct Gemini CLI calls with --prompt, --output-format json, and --approval-mode plan now work interactively. Cleaning up remaining wording ambiguity so docs and source comments distinguish Plugboard/plugin stdin from the Gemini subprocess invocation.
Clarified the final invocation model in source comments and docs: Plugboard delivers the message body to gemini-plugin on stdin, but gemini-plugin invokes Gemini as '--prompt <message body> --output-format json --approval-mode plan' and does not forward stdin to the Gemini subprocess. User confirmed that exact Gemini CLI shape works interactively.

title: Switch Gemini plugin to stream-json stdin mode
description: Update gemini-plugin to match RoboRev's working Gemini integration: pass the prompt via stdin, invoke gemini with --output-format stream-json and --approval-mode plan, parse streamed result text, and verify the real Plugboard workflow locally before pushing.
notes: Parked this experiment on branch experiment/gemini-stream-json at commit 92b6296. The standalone stream-json Gemini plugin worked in tests but the real Plugboard worker flow still timed out, so main stays on the simpler --prompt implementation for now.

title: API plugin architecture for hosted model backends
description: Implement an API-plugin path for Plugboard so workers can call hosted model or service APIs directly while preserving Plugboard’s topic-based, agent-agnostic architecture.

title: Define API plugin request and config shape
description: Define the API plugin boundaries for Plugboard workers: request and response mapping, error handling, timeout expectations, and configuration or environment model.

title: Implement first hosted API plugin
description: Implement the first practical API-backed plugin for Plugboard, either against a minimal hosted backend target or a small reusable framework that is concrete enough to validate the architecture.

title: Add tests for API plugin boundaries
description: Add tests for API plugin boundaries with mocked HTTP or service integrations where appropriate, without requiring live network calls for all test coverage.

title: Document API plugin authentication and configuration
description: Document environment variables, secrets handling expectations, safe local development guidance, and any backend-specific configuration needed for API plugins.

title: Define failure and retry behavior for API plugins
description: Document and, where appropriate, implement sensible behavior around HTTP or API failures, including timeout handling, retry boundaries, and what the worker should publish on failure.

title: Document API-backed worker workflows
description: Write the user-facing docs for running Plugboard with an API-backed worker: exact commands, request/reply workflow, and how this differs from stateless command plugins and local model plugins.

title: Add per-request metadata and Ollama model override
description: Add generic string metadata to messages, round-trip it through SQLite, expose request --meta KEY=VALUE, inject metadata into plugin env vars, and let ollama-plugin honor PLUGBOARD_META_MODEL before OLLAMA_PLUGIN_MODEL.

title: Define check ollama as stored-conversation follow-up
description: Make prompt-level check ollama use the most recent stored async Ollama conversation_id, not recent-replies listing. Update AGENTS/docs and separate it from the recent inbox helper.

title: Clarify worker lifecycle in README
description: Make the README explicit that request/reply requires a running worker, add a two-terminal example, and document worker lifecycle and troubleshooting.

title: Fast passive request-reply path for worker-hosted tools
description: Reduce activation latency and improve lifecycle semantics for passive-tool request/reply workflows. Add a one-shot worker mode, blocking wait/claim behavior that does not depend on idle polling sleeps, and a practical request/reply helper built on Plugboard topics and correlated follow-up messages.
notes:
Implemented notifier-backed blocking wait for the SQLite exchange and wired plugboard run --once through a real blocking claim path. The remaining open work is the request helper and latency-path documentation.
Persistent worker semantics now treat wakeups as advisory only: after a wake or successful handle, the worker drains currently claimable work until claim_next returns None, then blocks again. Reactive run --once remains one-message-only.
Implemented plugboard request as a thin publish-and-wait helper. It correlates replies by conversation_id plus success/failure topic, uses advisory wakeups with a pre-armed wait ticket, prints the reply body, and exits nonzero for failure-topic replies.

title: Implement blocking wait-and-claim for SQLite exchange
description: Add a blocking wait/claim path for the SQLite exchange so one-shot and passive request/reply workflows do not rely on idle polling sleeps. Evaluate SQLite-friendly options such as a local notification table plus condition-variable style wakeups or a file-descriptor backed wait primitive, while preserving atomic claim semantics.

title: Add request-reply helper command
description: Add an optional helper command that publishes a message and waits for the correlated reply topic or conversation follow-up, so passive tool exchanges do not require a permanently running worker or manual multi-terminal coordination.

title: Measure and document activation latency path
description: Document the current publish to claim to follow-up timing path, including runner startup overhead, idle polling behavior, and the expected latency improvements once one-shot blocking claim support exists.

title: Add one-shot worker mode
description: Implement plugboard run --once so the worker can block until one matching message exists, claim it immediately, execute the configured backend once, publish the correlated follow-up, and exit without loitering.

title: Harden persistent wakeup drain semantics
description: Align the worker implementation with the advisory wakeup semantics in DESIGN.md and ARCHITECTURE.md. Keep run --once one-message-only, but ensure persistent workers drain all currently claimable work after any wake or successful handle before blocking again.

title: Lease-based claim ownership for multi-worker pools
description: Harden claim ownership for concurrent worker pools by separating stable worker_group from ephemeral worker_instance_id and making stale-claim recovery depend on lease expiry in the transactional claim path rather than startup sweep.

title: Investigate and fix blocking wakeup path
description: Reproduce the notifier-backed blocking wakeup failure, fix the blocking wake/check semantics so workers reliably notice publishes, add regression coverage for run --once and persistent workers on fresh topics, and improve inspect/debug visibility for claims and stale ownership.

title: Improve wakeup path beyond bounded 250ms fallback
description: Track the remaining product gap in the current hybrid advisory wakeup plus bounded polling design. Document that correctness currently relies on 250 ms re-checks when notifier delivery fails, and follow up with a lower-latency local wakeup path that reduces worst-case activation latency without changing Plugboard core semantics.

title: Async operator experience and guidance
description: Reframe Plugboard around its true asynchronous operating model: publish work, continue doing other work, and read replies later. Cover shell/process lifecycle hygiene, async-first docs, inbox-style tutorials, Codex-facing guidance, foreground-tool differentiation, completion notification exploration, and a narrower local-model story.

title: Reframe docs around async-first Plugboard usage
description: Update README and core docs so the primary workflow is enqueue work now, continue doing other work, and read replies later. Present blocking request/reply only as a convenience for quick experiments.

title: Teach agents to capture request identifiers
description: Add clear AGENTS.md guidance that agents using plugboard request must capture publish-time identifiers, prefer --json when tooling reliability matters, and treat conversation_id as the primary handle for later async lookup.

title: Add agent-facing async tracking example
description: Add a concrete agent-oriented example showing request send, identifier capture, later conversation-based lookup, and reporting either a terminal reply or no reply yet.

title: Evaluate conversation-based check helper for agents
description: Evaluate whether a small helper or wrapper would make conversation-based async checking easier for agents and tools without adding unnecessary core surface area.

title: Teach agents to check replies by conversation_id
description: Document the standard agent follow-up path: send request, capture identifiers, later read or check by conversation_id, and determine whether a terminal success/failure reply exists.

title: Emphasize conversation_id as async retrieval unit
description: Tighten docs so topics are understood as routing and conversation_id as the natural unit for retrieving a request/reply exchange.

title: Document fallback when request IDs are unavailable
description: Document body-text matching as a heuristic fallback when stored request identifiers are unavailable, preferring the latest plausible request and making the ambiguity explicit.

title: Fix non-blocking Ollama send path
description: Make the Ollama async workflow use a non-blocking send path that captures identifiers at publish time and pairs cleanly with later conversation-based checking.

title: Document blocking ask vs non-blocking send
description: Update AGENTS and user docs so blocking ask-and-wait and non-blocking send/check are clearly distinct operator paths.

title: Add non-blocking Ollama send path
description: Keep blocking ask intact, and add a distinct non-blocking Ollama send/enqueue path that publishes immediately, supports per-request metadata, and returns identifiers for later conversation-based checking.

title: Teach human-friendly output for async Ollama flow
description: Update AGENTS and docs so JSON is used only for internal parsing and user-facing responses remain short plain text for send and check flows.

title: Add an async inbox tutorial
description: Create a concrete end-to-end tutorial showing request now, do something else, and later check inbox/read replies.

title: Teach non-blocking send ollama behavior
description: Update agent guidance so send ollama uses the non-blocking publish path, captures identifiers with --json internally, does not wait for job completion, and returns to the user immediately.

title: Differentiate Plugboard from foreground parallel tools
description: Add a short, measured explanation that Plugboard is a durable asynchronous exchange, not merely foreground parallel orchestration.

title: Explore completion notification ergonomics
description: Explore or define a future human notification path for completed jobs, likely as a design/doc task first.

title: Add Codex-facing Plugboard guidance
description: Document the preferred interaction pattern for agent/tool use: enqueue now and check later unless the user explicitly asks to block and wait.

title: Narrow the local-model story to bounded transforms
description: Document only useful bounded transforms for small local models and avoid overclaiming general usefulness for tiny models.

title: Fix shell and worker lifecycle hygiene
description: Investigate why Codex leaves shells or worker processes alive after use, document expected lifecycle for resident versus one-shot workers, and make safe cleanup/default behavior clearer where possible.

title: Add ergonomic ollama check helper
description: Add a small, repeatable helper for checking recent replies on ollama.done and ollama.failed so the async ask/check workflow is easier for humans and Codex to use.

title: Use message and conversation IDs for async request tracking
description: Make plugboard request return stable identifiers for agent use, and document the intended async workflow of sending work, capturing IDs, and later checking replies by conversation_id rather than fuzzy matching or latest-N heuristics.

title: Implement minimal Plugboard prototype
description:
Read the repository README.md and implement the smallest working prototype consistent with it.

Constraints:

- Plugboard is a local textual message exchange.
- Do not introduce agent abstractions or workflow engines.
- Do not introduce typed RPC protocols.
- Keep the system minimal and inspectable.
- Messages should be textual with simple metadata (for example topic, timestamp, id).

The first prototype should support:

- publishing a message
- listing or polling messages by topic
- claiming a message
- acknowledging completion
- publishing follow-up messages

Prefer simple local storage such as sqlite or files.

Keep the code small and readable.

title: Bootstrap Rust crate, dependencies, and project skeleton
description:
Create or update the project as a Rust binary crate with the expected top-level structure and minimal compilable scaffolding.

Required outcomes:
- Cargo.toml exists and is sane
- src/ tree exists
- module files exist with minimal scaffolding
- schema.sql exists, even if preliminary
- examples/ and tests/ directories exist
- the project compiles with placeholder logic

Dependency guidance:
Prefer a small set of boring dependencies only. Reasonable categories include:
- CLI parsing
- SQLite access
- time handling
- id generation
- error handling

Avoid:
- heavy async runtimes
- framework-like crates
- unnecessary serialization stacks unless clearly needed

Do not implement real business logic yet beyond what is needed for compilable scaffolding.

title: Add core unit tests for domain and exchange behaviour
description:
Add the main unit tests needed to keep the implementation honest.

Required outcomes:
- unit tests cover core logic around messages, claims, publish/read, claim transitions, and follow-up behaviour
- tests are located close to the logic where practical
- the test suite runs cleanly

Constraints:
- prefer focused tests over giant integration blobs
- do not rely only on manual verification

title: Add one small end-to-end integration test or example flow
description:
Add one compact demonstration that proves the core idea.

Required outcomes:
- either an integration test or an example program/script exists
- it shows a message being published
- a runner claims it
- a command executes
- a follow-up message is emitted
- the resulting conversation can be inspected

Constraints:
- keep the demo small
- do not build a showcase framework
- use an ordinary command if possible to keep the example understandable

title: Polish error handling, documentation comments, and final wiring
description:
Do the final cleanup needed for a credible v1 prototype.

Required outcomes:
- errors are reasonably readable
- module boundaries remain clear
- obvious rough edges in CLI or exchange wiring are cleaned up
- code comments or doc comments exist where they clarify intent
- the project builds and tests cleanly

Constraints:
- do not add new major features here
- keep polish proportional
- prefer clarity over cleverness

title: Define domain types for messages and claims
description:
Implement the small core domain types needed for v1.

Required outcomes:
- domain module exists
- Message type exists
- Claim type exists
- ClaimStatus enum exists
- constructors or helper functions exist where useful
- types reflect the architecture docs without adding extra concepts

Constraints:
- keep these types concrete and boring
- do not create Agent, Workflow, Job, Subscription, ExecutionPlan, or similar
- metadata may exist as an optional shallow field, but should not dominate the model
- message bodies are textual and primary

Tests:
- add unit tests for basic type construction where sensible

title: Create initial SQLite schema and database initialization
description:
Implement schema.sql and the database initialization path.

Required outcomes:
- schema.sql contains the initial messages and claims tables
- useful indexes exist, especially for topic and active claims
- exchange initialization can apply the schema
- local database setup is testable

Constraints:
- only messages and claims are durable tables in v1
- keep schema aligned with ARCHITECTURE.md
- do not add conversations table unless absolutely necessary
- do not add subscriptions, jobs, workflows, retries, schedules, or anything similar

Tests:
- add a test that initializes the schema successfully

title: Implement exchange publish and read operations
description:
Implement the exchange API for writing and reading messages.

Required outcomes:
- publish message operation exists
- read by topic exists
- read by conversation exists
- basic inspect/list support over messages exists
- conversation id propagation behaviour is implemented consistently with the architecture docs

Constraints:
- publishing appends a new message only
- publishing does not mutate prior messages
- if a message has no conversation_id, assign according to the architecture guidance
- follow-up messages should be able to inherit conversation context

Tests:
- publish/read round trip
- conversation id propagation
- reading by topic
- reading by conversation

title: Implement atomic claim logic by topic
description:
Implement transactional claiming of one eligible message for a topic.

Required outcomes:
- claim operation exists on the exchange API
- claiming is atomic and transactional
- a message with an active claim is not claimed twice
- the selection mechanism is simple and deterministic enough for v1

Constraints:
- do not implement list-then-claim as separate uncontrolled steps
- do not add priorities, fairness tuning, or scheduling
- an eligible message for v1 is simply one matching the topic with no active claim

Tests:
- first claim succeeds
- second concurrent-or-sequential attempt does not reclaim the same active message
- claiming non-existent eligible work behaves correctly

title: Implement claim completion, failure, and timeout transitions
description:
Implement the operational transitions for claims.

Required outcomes:
- complete claim operation exists
- fail claim operation exists
- timeout handling path exists at least at the exchange state transition level
- claim statuses transition cleanly
- completed_at is recorded where appropriate

Constraints:
- claim state is separate from message content
- do not mutate original messages
- keep state transitions explicit and small

Tests:
- complete transition
- fail transition
- timeout transition
- invalid transition handling if needed

title: Implement follow-up message creation for outcomes
description:
Implement the logic that appends follow-up messages after processing outcomes.

Required outcomes:
- success follow-up message creation exists
- failure follow-up message creation exists
- timeout follow-up message creation exists
- parent_id and conversation_id are propagated correctly

Recommended topic pattern:
- original topic: code.generate
- success: code.generated
- failure: code.generate.failed
- timeout: code.generate.timed_out

Constraints:
- append follow-up messages instead of mutating prior messages
- keep topic derivation simple
- do not build a workflow system around this

Tests:
- parent reference is correct
- conversation inheritance is correct
- topics are assigned correctly

title: Implement CLI commands for publish, read, and inspect
description:
Implement the first user-facing CLI commands as thin wrappers.

Required outcomes:
- publish command works
- read command works for topic and conversation
- inspect command works at a simple human-readable level
- main CLI wiring is coherent

Constraints:
- keep output simple and useful
- do not overdesign formatting
- CLI should call into application/exchange logic rather than embedding behaviour

Tests:
- add at least minimal integration coverage or command-level tests where practical

title: Implement the command runner loop
description:
Implement the minimal runner that turns a passive command into an asynchronous participant.

Required outcomes:
- run command accepts topic, success-topic, failure-topic, timeout, and child command
- runner polls for work
- runner claims one message
- runner passes message body to child stdin
- runner captures stdout, stderr, and exit status
- runner records claim outcome
- runner publishes follow-up output messages

Constraints:
- keep it single-message-at-a-time
- keep it synchronous in v1
- runner depends on exchange API, not SQL
- do not parse body text for routing decisions
- do not add concurrency pools, scheduling, retries, or backoff systems beyond a tiny sleep loop

Tests:
- success command path
- failing command path
- timeout path if practical

title: Map Gemini plugin message body to prompt
description: Update gemini-plugin so the claimed Plugboard message body is passed to Gemini as the --prompt value instead of stdin, add tests for the invocation contract, and document the rationale in source comments.
notes: Changed gemini-plugin so the Plugboard message body read from stdin becomes Gemini's --prompt value, with Gemini launched without forwarded stdin. Added integration and worker-host tests to verify the prompt mapping and the absence of stdin forwarding.

title: Add CI badge to README
description: Add a GitHub Actions CI badge to the README that links to the cargo-test workflow page.

title: Add GitHub Actions cargo test workflow
description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs cargo test for the repository on pushes and pull requests, and verify it matches the current Rust project layout.

title: Align Gemini plugin docs and source comments
description: Update the Gemini plugin source comments and repository docs so they describe the confirmed stdin plus --prompt Gemini invocation model accurately.
notes: Updated gemini-plugin source comments plus README and Gemini how-to docs so they describe the confirmed stdin plus --prompt invocation model and the current shell-validation caveat accurately.

title: Operationalize wakeup tuning and debug visibility
description: Add standard RUST_LOG-based debug logging for the blocking wakeup path and expose timeout/re-check intervals explicitly in CLI help/docs for worker and request/reply flows.

title: Clarify inspect as forensic command
description: Improve inspect CLI help and README guidance so inspect is clearly positioned as a debugging/forensic command rather than the normal way to view messages.

title: Worker Host and Plugin Architecture
description: Introduce a worker runtime and plugin system to enable asynchronous agent/tool execution on top of Plugboard topics, while keeping Plugboard core agent-agnostic.

title: Refactor run into worker host
description: Clarify worker responsibilities, isolate the polling and claim loop, and separate execution logic from CLI parsing so the runtime is a worker host rather than a generic subprocess helper.

title: Add practical quickstart and worker plugin docs
description: Add a short end-to-end quickstart and a concise how-to for writing worker plugins, then link both from the README without bloating the top-level project overview.

title: Remove markdown separators from README and architecture docs
description: Remove standalone horizontal-rule markdown separators from README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md to keep the documentation style cleaner and more consistent.

title: Define plugin interface
description: Introduce a minimal Rust plugin interface for worker execution. Input should include message data and context; output should be a structured success or failure result.

title: Implement command plugin
description: Preserve current stdin/stdout subprocess behavior as a command plugin. Ensure stdin is written then closed, stdout and stderr are captured, and exit codes are mapped into structured results.

title: Timeout and lifecycle handling
description: Enforce per-message timeout handling in the worker host and publish timeout follow-up messages on <topic>.timed_out.

title: Message to execution mapping
description: Define and document how message bodies and metadata map into execution inputs, including the decision to use stdin instead of arguments in the initial plugin contract.

title: Example plugin
description: Add a simple deterministic or API-backed example plugin that proves an end-to-end Plugboard topic to worker to plugin to follow-up flow.
notes: Added a deterministic example plugin binary at src/bin/example-review-plugin.rs, documented the local workflow in docs/quickstart.md and docs/howto/write-a-worker-plugin.md, and added an end-to-end integration test that runs the worker host against the example plugin binary.

title: Documentation alignment
description: Align README, architecture, and design documentation with the worker host and plugin architecture. Remove implications of identity-based delivery and add an end-to-end example.

title: CLI UX improvements
description: Clarify worker invocation in the CLI and help text, including whether to keep run or introduce a clearer worker-oriented command surface.

title: Handle BrokenPipe in command plugin stdin writes
description: The command plugin should not crash the worker host when a child command closes stdin early or ignores input. Treat BrokenPipe as a valid non-interactive command behavior and keep the plugin execution flow alive.

title: Refactor ollama model selection tests to avoid process env mutation
description: Address RoboRev finding on d8f1b3b by making ollama-plugin model selection testable without mutating process-global environment in unit tests.

title: Align check-ollama with plugboard read
description: Fix the Ollama check helper so it wraps the existing plugboard read command instead of querying SQLite directly, keeping the helper aligned with the documented layering.

title: Add real Gemini worker plugin
description: Implement a real Gemini adapter plugin for the worker host, document its prerequisites and request/reply workflow, keep the deterministic example plugin clearly marked as a demo, and add realistic tests around the adapter logic.
notes:
Implemented a real gemini-plugin binary that invokes the installed Gemini CLI once per message in non-interactive JSON mode, added parser and fake-CLI integration tests, kept example-review-plugin clearly marked as a demo, rewrote the Codex-to-Gemini guide around the real plugin and explicit prerequisites, and attempted a live Gemini run which timed out without producing a response in this environment.
Added src/bin/gemini-plugin.rs as a real Gemini CLI adapter, added fake-CLI unit and end-to-end coverage, split docs so example-review-plugin stays clearly demo-only, and documented real Gemini prerequisites. Direct non-interactive Gemini CLI probes still timed out without output in this environment, so live end-to-end verification remains dependent on local Gemini auth/network health.

title: Improve human-readable CLI timestamps and visibility rules
description: Use local time, time-first formatting, and no fractional seconds in human-readable Plugboard output; keep JSON unchanged; document mandatory CLI-output visibility for timing-sensitive workflows in AGENTS.

title: Local model plugin for fast Plugboard demos
description: Implement a local-model plugin path so Plugboard can demonstrate useful, low-latency request/reply workflows without depending on slow cold starts from hosted agent CLIs. Include full documentation for setting up a local model and using it through Plugboard.

title: Implement local model plugin adapter
description: Implement a plugin or adapter that sends claimed message text to the chosen local model backend, prints the final result to stdout, and returns nonzero on failure while fitting Plugboard's worker lifecycle.

title: Select and justify local model backend approach
description: Choose the pragmatic local model/runtime approach for Plugboard demos, justify the choice, and prefer something realistic for developers to run locally. The resulting work should set up the rest of the local-model path and explicitly optimize for low-latency local request/reply workflows.
notes: Selected Ollama as the local backend path and defaulted the plugin to gemma3:1b. This keeps the path realistic for developers, low-latency enough for local demos, and easy to verify independently with ollama serve plus the local /api/generate endpoint.

title: Document local model installation and setup
description: Write a full tutorial for a developer starting from scratch: prerequisites, installation steps, model pull or load steps, local runtime startup or preparation, and an independent verification check before Plugboard is introduced.

title: Add example local model workflow guide
description: Add a short practical walkthrough from publish to worker to read reply for the chosen local model path. Keep it concise, runnable, and aligned with the fuller installation and Plugboard integration tutorials.

title: Add tests for local model plugin boundaries
description: Add tests for the local model plugin's adapter logic and process boundaries where practical. The test strategy should not require live external network access for every test.

title: Document Plugboard integration for local model backend
description: Write the full tutorial for using the chosen local model through Plugboard: exact commands, worker invocation, request/reply workflow, expected outputs, and troubleshooting around local runtime readiness and worker timeouts.

title: Add request conversation debugging aids
description: Add a debug log with published request id and conversation_id, and expose a clearer read --conversation-id alias for inspecting a specific request/reply conversation.

title: Address RoboRev findings for conversation check helper
Add timestamps to human-readable CLI output
description: Show ISO 8601 UTC timestamps in human-readable output for publish/check, and read if it stays small. Keep --json output unchanged and avoid schema or behavior changes.

title: Address RoboRev findings for Gemini plugin
description: Add the remaining Gemini adapter fallback-path tests called out by RoboRev and reduce duplicated prerequisite text across the Gemini docs.
notes: Addressed RoboRev jobs 192, 193, and 195 by adding Gemini adapter fallback-path tests and deduplicating the Gemini prerequisite docs. Follow-up review job 197 passed with no findings.

title: Improve CLI help and document agent-style topic workflows
description: Make Plugboard understandable from the CLI by improving help text, adding a concrete Codex-to-Gemini style request/reply how-to, aligning docs, and verifying the documented topic-based workflow locally.
notes: Improved CLI help text for publish/read/run and top-level help, added a Codex-to-Gemini request/reply how-to using topic conventions and the local example plugin, aligned README/quickstart/architecture/design docs, and will verify the documented workflow with the built binaries.

title: Clarify stateless worker execution model in help and docs
description: Make it explicit in CLI help and practical docs that worker backends are invoked once per message, must read stdin, write stdout, and exit, and that Plugboard does not manage persistent sessions.
notes: Clarified that worker backends are stateless per-message executors in run help and practical docs: one fresh process per message, stdin/stdout/exit contract, no shared memory between runs, and no persistent sessions managed by Plugboard.
